headline: H100 benchmarks show DFlash speculative serving improves throughput over target-only serving, with the strongest observed run reaching 1.631x output-token speedup.

runs.label: Qwen3.5 4B baseline
runs.notes: Target-only H100 baseline for the Qwen3.5 4B comparison.

runs.label: Qwen3.5 4B DFlash
runs.notes: DFlash improved output throughput from 2349.324 to 3832.692 tok/s and reduced p50/p95 latency.

runs.label: Qwen3 4B baseline
runs.notes: Target-only H100 baseline for the Qwen3 4B comparison.

runs.label: Qwen3 4B DFlash
runs.notes: DFlash improved output throughput from 4017.917 to 4324.385 tok/s; p50 improved while p95 regressed.

runs.label: RTX 4090 slot
runs.notes: Reserved for BF16-capable consumer GPU testing with the cheap profile.

milestones.title: H100 Qwen3.5 4B comparison
milestones.body: DFlash reached 3832.692 tok/s vs 2349.324 tok/s baseline, a 1.631x throughput speedup.

milestones.title: H100 Qwen3 4B comparison
milestones.body: DFlash reached 4324.385 tok/s vs 4017.917 tok/s baseline, a 1.076x throughput speedup.

milestones.title: RTX 24GB validation
milestones.body: Run the same baseline and DFlash scripts on RTX 3090/4090-class BF16-capable hardware.

milestones.title: vLLM path
milestones.body: SGLang is the first supported path here; vLLM DFlash remains experimental depending on the installed build.

decisions: Lead with measured speedup, not placeholder run cards.
Publish compact H100 comparison summaries in docs/data/summary.json while keeping raw results under ignored results/.
Leave explicit space for future RTX 3090/4090, A10G, L4, and other BF16-capable GPU tests.
Use the same prompts, max_new_tokens=128, and concurrency=32 when comparing baseline and DFlash on H100.
